title: Auto-generated infra image for Podman 4.7.1 is missing catatonit binary
body:
### Issue Description

When creating and starting a new pod using Podman 4.7.1, the infra container running the auto-generated `localhost/podman-pause:4.7.1-1696583554` fails to start and exits with a message indicating that `/catatonit` is missing:

```
{"msg":"exec container process (missing dynamic library?) `/catatonit`: No such file or directory","level":"error","time":"2023-10-07T13:43:19.159911Z"}
```

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue:

1. `podman pod create --replace test`

3. `podman ps -a`

```
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                                    COMMAND     CREATED         STATUS      PORTS       NAMES
26969fee4423  localhost/podman-pause:4.7.1-1696583554              14 seconds ago  Created                 e3c3b255ac37-infra
```

4. `podman pod start test`

5. `podman ps -a`

```
podman ps -a
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                                    COMMAND     CREATED         STATUS                     PORTS       NAMES
26969fee4423  localhost/podman-pause:4.7.1-1696583554              53 seconds ago  Exited (1) 16 seconds ago              e3c3b255ac37-infra
```

6. `podman pod logs test`

```
{"msg":"exec container process (missing dynamic library?) `/catatonit`: No such file or directory","level":"error","time":"2023-10-07T13:43:19.159911Z"}
```


### Describe the results you received

I expect the infra container to be running after executing `podman pod start test`.

### Describe the results you expected

The infra container for the pod `test` fails to start with an error message indicating that `/catatonit` is missing.

The problem did not occur with Podman 4.6.2. Since, I was running Podman 4.6.2 before upgrading to 4.7.1, the infra image for 4.6.2 was available on my machine. Creating and running a pod with the 4.6.2 infra image does not fail:

1. `podman pod create --replace --infra-image localhost/podman-pause:4.6.2-1693343961 test`

2. `podman pod start test`

3. `podman ps -a`

```
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                                    COMMAND     CREATED         STATUS        PORTS       NAMES
7fd859a866cb  localhost/podman-pause:4.6.2-1693343961              13 seconds ago  Up 6 seconds              f5ae2cdcf723-infra
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

I am running Arch Linux with up-to-date packages. The latest [podman](https://archlinux.org/packages/extra/x86_64/podman/) and [catatonit](https://archlinux.org/packages/extra/x86_64/catatonit/) packages are installed:

comments.body:
My system is running **Arch Linux** with kernel version **6.5.5-zen1-1-zen** and podman version **4.7.1**

```bash
$ podman version
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.7.1
API Version:  4.7.1
Go Version:   go1.21.1
Git Commit:   ef83eeb9c7482826672f3efa12db3d61c88df6c4-dirty
Built:        Fri Oct  6 14:42:34 2023
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

- I saved the infra image to an oci format archive and unpacked it

```bash
$ podman save > pause.tar podman-pause:4.7.1-1696583554 --format oci-archive
```

```bash
$ tar -xf pause.tar ; eza -al --icons --tree pause

🗀 .
├── 🗀 blobs
│  └── 🗀 sha256
│     ├── ⬤ 8d9f6917cce0fbfd3307c46785e2a241ccc7b994750cafa835329cbbd9f45066
│     ├── ⬤ 90af5c2aafe329f58a2877eca50fd00cf0d78b9c713e58a1a7b60b1c808cb23a
│     └── ⬤ 86773bc7ca0a41bb4a14169eefbbb6b98fa069df5c30a0985b26663572e7fddb
├── {} index.json
└──  oci-layout
``` 
- The binary is indeed present inside the archive

```bash
$ tar -xf 90af5c2aafe329f58a2877eca50fd00cf0d78b9c713e58a1a7b60b1c808cb23a

$ ls -l

-rw-r--r-- 1 arch arch  668 Oct  7 22:28 86773bc7ca0a41bb4a14169eefbbb6b98fa069df5c30a0985b26663572e7fddb
-rw-r--r-- 1 arch arch  500 Oct  7 22:28 8d9f6917cce0fbfd3307c46785e2a241ccc7b994750cafa835329cbbd9f45066
-rw-r--r-- 1 arch arch 6.1K Oct  7 22:28 90af5c2aafe329f58a2877eca50fd00cf0d78b9c713e58a1a7b60b1c808cb23a
-rwxr-xr-x 1 arch arch  18K Oct  4 22:20 catatonit
``` 

- The latest version of catatonit present in the arch repository is **0.2.0-1**, replacing it with the older version **[catatonit-0.1.7-2-x86_64.pkg.tar.zst](https://archive.archlinux.org/packages/c/catatonit/catatonit-0.1.7-2-x86_64.pkg.tar.zst)** works

```bash
$ pacman -Qi catatonit

Name            : catatonit
Version         : 0.1.7-2
Description     : A container init that is so simple it's effectively brain-dead.
Architecture    : x86_64
URL             : https://github.com/openSUSE/catatonit/
Licenses        : GPL3
Groups          : None
Provides        : None
Depends On      : None
Optional Deps   : None
Required By     : podman
Optional For    : None
Conflicts With  : None
Replaces        : None
Installed Size  : 775.88 KiB
Packager        : David Runge <dvzrv@archlinux.org>
Build Date      : Tue Jul 26 23:29:57 2022
Install Date    : Sat Oct 7 22:50:34 2023
Install Reason  : Explicitly installed
Install Script  : No
Validated By    : None
```

- The infra container successfully running with catatonit version 0.1.7-2

```bash
$ podman pod create --replace test ; podman pod start test ; podman ps -a
91756aa0a2a65bb3fc0b1d5bcf48e97f029e9ccb90083bd7a1412557b398a3e1
91756aa0a2a65bb3fc0b1d5bcf48e97f029e9ccb90083bd7a1412557b398a3e1
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                                    COMMAND     CREATED                 STATUS       PORTS       NAMES
e01d7cc150ca  localhost/podman-pause:4.7.1-1696583554              Less than a second ago  Up 1 second              91756aa0a2a6-infra
```
***See the last commit history for [catatonit](https://gitlab.archlinux.org/archlinux/packaging/packages/catatonit/-/commit/3292384249af8165975586adb26994928ec6cca1) package in arch repository***
Sadly downgrading catatonit on my arch doesn't fix this issue killing every pod on my server on startup.

I've got the same issue on Arch. After downgrading Podman or Cataonit I've got the same issue.

```
testing@meterkast ~]$ pacman -Q | grep -E "podman|catatonit"
catatonit 0.2.0-1
podman 4.7.1-1

[testing@meterkast ~]$ podman pod create testpod
e8fd1f42054a2f03bcbef9f899b0167bbfe0dfe56e41353f67644a9817f9040d

[testing@meterkast ~]$ podman ps -a
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                                    COMMAND     CREATED         STATUS      PORTS       NAMES
cfd4736208eb  localhost/podman-pause:4.7.1-1696583554              12 seconds ago  Created                 e8fd1f42054a-infra

[testing@meterkast ~]$ podman pod start testpod
e8fd1f42054a2f03bcbef9f899b0167bbfe0dfe56e41353f67644a9817f9040d

[testing@meterkast ~]$ podman ps -a
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                                    COMMAND     CREATED         STATUS                    PORTS       NAMES
cfd4736208eb  localhost/podman-pause:4.7.1-1696583554              24 seconds ago  Exited (1) 3 seconds ago              e8fd1f42054a-infra

[testing@meterkast ~]$ podman pod logs testpod
{"msg":"exec container process (missing dynamic library?) `/catatonit`: No such file or directory","level":"error","time":"2023-10-08T06:37:08.935680Z"}
```

After downgrading catatonit it still does not work on my side:

```
[root@meterkast ~]# pacman -U /var/cache/pacman/pkg/catatonit-0.1.7-2-x86_64.pkg.tar.zst
loading packages...
warning: downgrading package catatonit (0.2.0-1 => 0.1.7-2)
resolving dependencies...
..snip..
```

```
[testing@meterkast ~]$ pacman -Q | grep catatonit
catatonit 0.1.7-2

[testing@meterkast ~]$ podman pod create barpod
c92625c1d851644737cf9504e65465b2215e0c8e07568370be05d93cc71d7671

[testing@meterkast ~]$ podman pod start barpod
c92625c1d851644737cf9504e65465b2215e0c8e07568370be05d93cc71d7671

[testing@meterkast ~]$ podman ps -a
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                                         COMMAND     CREATED        STATUS                    PORTS       NAMES
cfd4736208eb  localhost/podman-pause:4.7.1-1696583554                   6 minutes ago  Exited (1) 5 minutes ago              e8fd1f42054a-infra
54074b698e2a  localhost/podman-pause:4.7.1-1696583554                   7 seconds ago  Exited (1) 2 seconds ago              c92625c1d851-infra

[testing@meterkast ~]$ podman pod ps
POD ID        NAME        STATUS      CREATED         INFRA ID      # OF CONTAINERS
c92625c1d851  barpod      Exited      24 seconds ago  54074b698e2a  1
e8fd1f42054a  testpod     Exited      6 minutes ago   cfd4736208eb  1

[testing@meterkast ~]$ podman pod logs barpod
{"msg":"exec container process (missing dynamic library?) `/catatonit`: No such file or directory","level":"error","time":"2023-10-08T06:43:05.528246Z"}
```

As a workaround I use the ubi pause image for now:

```
[testing@meterkast ~]$ podman pod create --infra-image registry.access.redhat.com/ubi8/pause foopod
7e7bebbef8936084d8fac11728d6a5b70fc3aa861170e3412f1f0327f849a770

[testing@meterkast ~]$ podman pod start foopod
7e7bebbef8936084d8fac11728d6a5b70fc3aa861170e3412f1f0327f849a770

[testing@meterkast ~]$ podman ps -a
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                                         COMMAND     CREATED         STATUS                    PORTS       NAMES
cd84db7858b1  registry.access.redhat.com/ubi8/pause:latest  infinity    50 seconds ago  Up 7 seconds                          7e7bebbef893-infra
```

After downgrading the cataonit package, it is essential to halt all pods and remove the pause container infrastructure image (localhost/podman-pause:4.7.1-1696583554) generated by podman. Upon the subsequent execution, podman will generate a new infrastructure image using the downgraded version of cataonit.

Thanks @Devbrat-Dev, that works:

```
[testing@meterkast ~]$ podman images
REPOSITORY                             TAG               IMAGE ID      CREATED         SIZE
localhost/podman-pause                 4.7.1-1696583554  d814ccf17d95  23 minutes ago  21.6 kB
registry.access.redhat.com/ubi8/pause  latest            1f8c5ed0ad54  3 weeks ago     28.5 MB

[testing@meterkast ~]$ podman rmi 1f8c5ed0ad54 d814ccf17d95
Untagged: registry.access.redhat.com/ubi8/pause:latest
Untagged: localhost/podman-pause:4.7.1-1696583554
Deleted: 1f8c5ed0ad54a922dd720dbf20292f1854173505a8e211dbc4e6fc3715514f1d
Deleted: d814ccf17d95d5beec51b07e3ab31921a74db96865aac038c46d1b3b250b6d85

[testing@meterkast ~]$ podman ps -a
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE       COMMAND     CREATED     STATUS      PORTS       NAMES

[testing@meterkast ~]$ pacman -Q | grep catatonit
catatonit 0.1.7-2

[testing@meterkast ~]$ podman pod create fooo
5d1abd539f0bb5cfaedc4952cebdbae29bfef26ee3ee4943658079d1ce6f7b76

[testing@meterkast ~]$ podman pod start fooo
5d1abd539f0bb5cfaedc4952cebdbae29bfef26ee3ee4943658079d1ce6f7b76

[testing@meterkast ~]$ podman pod ps
POD ID        NAME        STATUS      CREATED        INFRA ID      # OF CONTAINERS
5d1abd539f0b  fooo        Running     7 seconds ago  e5e931053ad2  1
```

Welcome  @juliu-s

This issue needs to be closed now as it is related to cataonit, the resolution process is currently underway for archlinux package [FS#79880](https://bugs.archlinux.org/task/79880)

I can confirm that the issue disappears after upgrading to [catatonit 0.2.0-3](https://gitlab.archlinux.org/archlinux/packaging/packages/catatonit/-/commit/7767fd07852809a327f355e8e60bf6702b566a5f).

Note that after upgrading from [catatonit 0.2.0-1](https://gitlab.archlinux.org/archlinux/packaging/packages/catatonit/-/commit/3292384249af8165975586adb26994928ec6cca1) to [catatonit 0.2.0-3](https://gitlab.archlinux.org/archlinux/packaging/packages/catatonit/-/commit/7767fd07852809a327f355e8e60bf6702b566a5f), you have to remove the old infra image with the faulty catatonit binary. To do this, run `podman image rm localhost/podman-pause:4.7.1-1696583554`. This infra image (with the new catatonit binary) will be regenerated when running `podman pod create` (because the `--infra` flag defaults to `true`).

Thanks everyone for all the comments, and sorry to the Podman maintainers for crowding the issue tracker with an Arch Linux-specific bug.
